assertions.title: AccountingService decomposed

steps.action: Count lines for accounting service classes under `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/accounting/service` using a Python scan.
steps.expected: Focused accounting services are all under 500 lines.
steps.observed: Multiple classes exceed 500 lines: AccountingCoreEngine.java (6212), AccountingFacade.java (2160), AccountingPeriodService.java (1006), ReconciliationService.java (811), AccountingAuditTrailService.java (526).

steps.action: Compare measured file sizes against the assertion threshold.
steps.expected: No accounting service class breaches the 500-line limit.
steps.observed: Threshold is not met on current branch, so decomposition criterion is not satisfied.

assertions.issues: Assertion requires focused accounting services under 500 lines; several accounting service classes remain above limit.

steps.action: Run targeted reconciliation test command: `mvn --settings /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/erp-domain/.mvn/settings.xml -f /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/erp-domain/pom.xml test -Djacoco.skip=true -Dtest=ReconciliationServiceTest`.
steps.expected: Bank reconciliation, subledger-to-GL reconciliation, and inter-company reconciliation flows pass with discrepancy reporting.
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; ReconciliationServiceTest ran 5 tests with 0 failures/0 errors, including bank, subledger, and two inter-company scenarios (matched + unmatched).

steps.action: Inspect reconciliation service and tests for inter-company flow coverage.
steps.expected: Inter-company reconcile API and matched/unmatched test coverage are present.
steps.observed: `ReconciliationService.interCompanyReconcile(...)` exists, and test cases `interCompanyReconcile_bidirectionalBalancesMatch_reportsMatchedItems` and `interCompanyReconcile_mismatchAndMissingCounterparty_reportsUnmatchedItems` are present and passed.

commands: grep/read inspection for "interCompanyReconcile" in ReconciliationService.java and ReconciliationServiceTest.java

assertions.title: Shared validation utilities

steps.action: Inspect shared validation utility implementation in `ValidationUtils`.
steps.expected: Shared utility provides positive checks, enum parsing with error messages, date-range validation, and null guards.

steps.action: Search module code usage for `ValidationUtils.parseEnum(...)` and `ValidationUtils.validateDateRange(...)`.
steps.expected: Shared enum/date-range utilities are reused across modules.
steps.observed: No matches found for either `parseEnum` or `validateDateRange` in module source paths.

steps.action: Scan module directories for any ValidationUtils usage footprint.
steps.expected: All modules show shared validation utility adoption.
steps.observed: Several modules use ValidationUtils (e.g., accounting, inventory, sales), but demo, portal, and reports modules show no ValidationUtils usage in current source tree.

commands: python3 module scan for ValidationUtils usage by module directory
evidence.output: parseEnum usage: none; validateDateRange usage: none; module scan: demo=no-usage, portal=no-usage, reports=no-usage

assertions.issues: Shared utility exists but broad adoption criteria remains incomplete: enum/date-range helper methods are unused in module paths, and not all module directories reference ValidationUtils.

frictions.description: Targeted Maven execution still triggers full test-source compilation before running a narrow class, adding runtime overhead.
frictions.resolution: Kept validation scope to a single assertion-specific test class (`ReconciliationServiceTest`) plus grep/read inspections to minimize runtime while preserving concrete evidence.

frictions.description: No running app service was required for these rerun assertions because evidence is structural (line counts/usage scans) and executable via isolated unit tests, not HTTP flow behavior.
frictions.resolution: Used targeted `mvn test` and source inspections only; did not start or restart shared infrastructure.

summary: Tested 3 rerun assertions: 1 passed (VAL-ACCT-008) and 2 failed (VAL-ACCT-001, VAL-FOUND-003). Evidence came from targeted reconciliation unit tests plus source-based structural/usage inspections.
